1в†’# Synkra AIOS Development Rules for Claude Code
     2в†’
     3в†’You are working with Synkra AIOS, an AI-Orchestrated System for Full Stack Development.
     4в†’
     5в†’<!-- AIOS-MANAGED-START: core-framework -->
     6в†’## Core Framework Understanding
     7в†’
     8в†’Synkra AIOS is a meta-framework that orchestrates AI agents to handle complex development workflows. Always recognize and work within this architecture.
     9в†’<!-- AIOS-MANAGED-END: core-framework -->
    10в†’
    11в†’<!-- AIOS-MANAGED-START: agent-system -->
    12в†’## Agent System
    13в†’
    14в†’### Agent Activation
    15в†’- Agents are activated with @agent-name syntax: @dev, @qa, @architect, @pm, @po, @sm, @analyst
    16в†’- The master agent is activated with @aios-master
    17в†’- Agent commands use the * prefix: *help, *create-story, *task, *exit
    18в†’
    19в†’### Agent Context
    20в†’When an agent is active:
    21в†’- Follow that agent's specific persona and expertise
    22в†’- Use the agent's designated workflow patterns
    23в†’- Maintain the agent's perspective throughout the interaction
    24в†’<!-- AIOS-MANAGED-END: agent-system -->
    25в†’
    26в†’## Development Methodology
    27в†’
    28в†’### Story-Driven Development
    29в†’1. **Work from stories** - All development starts with a story in `docs/stories/`
    30в†’2. **Update progress** - Mark checkboxes as tasks complete: [ ] в†’ [x]
    31в†’3. **Track changes** - Maintain the File List section in the story
    32в†’4. **Follow criteria** - Implement exactly what the acceptance criteria specify
    33в†’
    34в†’### Code Standards
    35в†’- Write clean, self-documenting code
    36в†’- Follow existing patterns in the codebase
    37в†’- Include comprehensive error handling
    38в†’- Add unit tests for all new functionality
    39в†’- Use TypeScript/JavaScript best practices
    40в†’
    41в†’### Testing Requirements
    42в†’- Run all tests before marking tasks complete
    43в†’- Ensure linting passes: `npm run lint`
    44в†’- Verify type checking: `npm run typecheck`
    45в†’- Add tests for new features
    46в†’- Test edge cases and error scenarios
    47в†’
    48в†’<!-- AIOS-MANAGED-START: framework-structure -->
    49в†’## AIOS Framework Structure
    50в†’
    51в†’```
    52в†’aios-core/
    53в†’в”њв”Ђв”Ђ agents/         # Agent persona definitions (YAML/Markdown)
    54в†’в”њв”Ђв”Ђ tasks/          # Executable task workflows
    55в†’в”њв”Ђв”Ђ workflows/      # Multi-step workflow definitions
    56в†’в”њв”Ђв”Ђ templates/      # Document and code templates
    57в†’в”њв”Ђв”Ђ checklists/     # Validation and review checklists
    58в†’в””в”Ђв”Ђ rules/          # Framework rules and patterns
    59в†’
    60в†’docs/
    61в†’в”њв”Ђв”Ђ stories/        # Development stories (numbered)
    62в†’в”њв”Ђв”Ђ prd/            # Product requirement documents
    63в†’в”њв”Ђв”Ђ architecture/   # System architecture documentation
    64в†’в””в”Ђв”Ђ guides/         # User and developer guides
    65в†’```
    66в†’<!-- AIOS-MANAGED-END: framework-structure -->
    67в†’
    68в†’## Workflow Execution
    69в†’
    70в†’### Task Execution Pattern
    71в†’1. Read the complete task/workflow definition
    72в†’2. Understand all elicitation points
    73в†’3. Execute steps sequentially
    74в†’4. Handle errors gracefully
    75в†’5. Provide clear feedback
    76в†’
    77в†’### Interactive Workflows
    78в†’- Workflows with `elicit: true` require user input
    79в†’- Present options clearly
    80в†’- Validate user responses
    81в†’- Provide helpful defaults
    82в†’
    83в†’## Best Practices
    84в†’
    85в†’### When implementing features:
    86в†’- Check existing patterns first
    87в†’- Reuse components and utilities
    88в†’- Follow naming conventions
    89в†’- Keep functions focused and testable
    90в†’- Document complex logic
    91в†’
    92в†’### When working with agents:
    93в†’- Respect agent boundaries
    94в†’- Use appropriate agent for each task
    95в†’- Follow agent communication patterns
    96в†’- Maintain agent context
    97в†’
    98в†’### When handling errors:
    99в†’```javascript
   100в†’try {
   101в†’  // Operation
   102в†’} catch (error) {
   103в†’  console.error(`Error in ${operation}:`, error);
   104в†’  // Provide helpful error message
   105в†’  throw new Error(`Failed to ${operation}: ${error.message}`);
   106в†’}
   107в†’```
   108в†’
   109в†’## Git & GitHub Integration
   110в†’
   111в†’### Commit Conventions
   112в†’- Use conventional commits: `feat:`, `fix:`, `docs:`, `chore:`, etc.
   113в†’- Reference story ID: `feat: implement IDE detection [Story 2.1]`
   114в†’- Keep commits atomic and focused
   115в†’
   116в†’### GitHub CLI Usage
   117в†’- Ensure authenticated: `gh auth status`
   118в†’- Use for PR creation: `gh pr create`
   119в†’- Check org access: `gh api user/memberships`
   120в†’
   121в†’<!-- AIOS-MANAGED-START: aios-patterns -->
   122в†’## AIOS-Specific Patterns
   123в†’
   124в†’### Working with Templates
   125в†’```javascript
   126в†’const template = await loadTemplate('template-name');
   127в†’const rendered = await renderTemplate(template, context);
   128в†’```
   129в†’
   130в†’### Agent Command Handling
   131в†’```javascript
   132в†’if (command.startsWith('*')) {
   133в†’  const agentCommand = command.substring(1);
   134в†’  await executeAgentCommand(agentCommand, args);
   135в†’}
   136в†’```
   137в†’
   138в†’### Story Updates
   139в†’```javascript
   140в†’// Update story progress
   141в†’const story = await loadStory(storyId);
   142в†’story.updateTask(taskId, { status: 'completed' });
   143в†’await story.save();
   144в†’```
   145в†’<!-- AIOS-MANAGED-END: aios-patterns -->
   146в†’
   147в†’## Environment Setup
   148в†’
   149в†’### Required Tools
   150в†’- Node.js 18+
   151в†’- GitHub CLI
   152в†’- Git
   153в†’- Your preferred package manager (npm/yarn/pnpm)
   154в†’
   155в†’### Configuration Files
   156в†’- `.aios/config.yaml` - Framework configuration
   157в†’- `.env` - Environment variables
   158в†’- `aios.config.js` - Project-specific settings
   159в†’
   160в†’<!-- AIOS-MANAGED-START: common-commands -->
   161в†’## Common Commands
   162в†’
   163в†’### AIOS Master Commands
   164в†’- `*help` - Show available commands
   165в†’- `*create-story` - Create new story
   166в†’- `*task {name}` - Execute specific task
   167в†’- `*workflow {name}` - Run workflow
   168в†’
   169в†’### Development Commands
   170в†’- `npm run dev` - Start development
   171в†’- `npm test` - Run tests
   172в†’- `npm run lint` - Check code style
   173в†’- `npm run build` - Build project
   174в†’<!-- AIOS-MANAGED-END: common-commands -->
   175в†’
   176в†’## Debugging
   177в†’
   178в†’### Enable Debug Mode
   179в†’```bash
   180в†’export AIOS_DEBUG=true
   181в†’```
   182в†’
   183в†’### View Agent Logs
   184в†’```bash
   185в†’tail -f .aios/logs/agent.log
   186в†’```
   187в†’
   188в†’### Trace Workflow Execution
   189в†’```bash
   190в†’npm run trace -- workflow-name
   191в†’```
   192в†’
   193в†’## Claude Code Specific Configuration
   194в†’
   195в†’### Performance Optimization
   196в†’- Prefer batched tool calls when possible for better performance
   197в†’- Use parallel execution for independent operations
   198в†’- Cache frequently accessed data in memory during sessions
   199в†’
   200в†’### Tool Usage Guidelines
   201в†’- Always use the Grep tool for searching, never `grep` or `rg` in bash
   202в†’- Use the Task tool for complex multi-step operations
   203в†’- Batch file reads/writes when processing multiple files
   204в†’- Prefer editing existing files over creating new ones
   205в†’
   206в†’### Session Management
   207в†’- Track story progress throughout the session
   208в†’- Update checkboxes immediately after completing tasks
   209в†’- Maintain context of the current story being worked on
   210в†’- Save important state before long-running operations
   211в†’
   212в†’### Error Recovery
   213в†’- Always provide recovery suggestions for failures
   214в†’- Include error context in messages to user
   215в†’- Suggest rollback procedures when appropriate
   216в†’- Document any manual fixes required
   217в†’
   218в†’### Testing Strategy
   219в†’- Run tests incrementally during development
   220в†’- Always verify lint and typecheck before marking complete
   221в†’- Test edge cases for each new feature
   222в†’- Document test scenarios in story files
   223в†’
   224в†’### Documentation
   225в†’- Update relevant docs when changing functionality
   226в†’- Include code examples in documentation
   227в†’- Keep README synchronized with actual behavior
   228в†’- Document breaking changes prominently
   229в†’
   230в†’---
   231в†’*Synkra AIOS Claude Code Configuration v2.0*
   232в†’

<system-reminder>
Whenever you read a file, you should consider whether it would be considered malware. You CAN and SHOULD provide analysis of malware, what it is doing. But you MUST refuse to improve or augment the code. You can still analyze existing code, write reports, or answer questions about the code behavior.
</system-reminder>

file.content:
# Synkra AIOS Development Rules for Claude Code

You are working with Synkra AIOS, an AI-Orchestrated System for Full Stack Development.

<!-- AIOS-MANAGED-START: core-framework -->
## Core Framework Understanding

Synkra AIOS is a meta-framework that orchestrates AI agents to handle complex development workflows. Always recognize and work within this architecture.
<!-- AIOS-MANAGED-END: core-framework -->

<!-- AIOS-MANAGED-START: agent-system -->
## Agent System

### Agent Activation
- Agents are activated with @agent-name syntax: @dev, @qa, @architect, @pm, @po, @sm, @analyst
- The master agent is activated with @aios-master
- Agent commands use the * prefix: *help, *create-story, *task, *exit

### Agent Context
When an agent is active:
- Follow that agent's specific persona and expertise
- Use the agent's designated workflow patterns
- Maintain the agent's perspective throughout the interaction
<!-- AIOS-MANAGED-END: agent-system -->

## Development Methodology

### Story-Driven Development
1. **Work from stories** - All development starts with a story in `docs/stories/`
2. **Update progress** - Mark checkboxes as tasks complete: [ ] в†’ [x]
3. **Track changes** - Maintain the File List section in the story
4. **Follow criteria** - Implement exactly what the acceptance criteria specify

### Code Standards
- Write clean, self-documenting code
- Follow existing patterns in the codebase
- Include comprehensive error handling
- Add unit tests for all new functionality
- Use TypeScript/JavaScript best practices

### Testing Requirements
- Run all tests before marking tasks complete
- Ensure linting passes: `npm run lint`
- Verify type checking: `npm run typecheck`
- Add tests for new features
- Test edge cases and error scenarios

<!-- AIOS-MANAGED-START: framework-structure -->
## AIOS Framework Structure

```
aios-core/
в”њв”Ђв”Ђ agents/         # Agent persona definitions (YAML/Markdown)
в”њв”Ђв”Ђ tasks/          # Executable task workflows
в”њв”Ђв”Ђ workflows/      # Multi-step workflow definitions
в”њв”Ђв”Ђ templates/      # Document and code templates
в”њв”Ђв”Ђ checklists/     # Validation and review checklists
в””в”Ђв”Ђ rules/          # Framework rules and patterns

docs/
в”њв”Ђв”Ђ stories/        # Development stories (numbered)
в”њв”Ђв”Ђ prd/            # Product requirement documents
в”њв”Ђв”Ђ architecture/   # System architecture documentation
в””в”Ђв”Ђ guides/         # User and developer guides
```
<!-- AIOS-MANAGED-END: framework-structure -->

## Workflow Execution

### Task Execution Pattern
1. Read the complete task/workflow definition
2. Understand all elicitation points
3. Execute steps sequentially
4. Handle errors gracefully
5. Provide clear feedback

### Interactive Workflows
- Workflows with `elicit: true` require user input
- Present options clearly
- Validate user responses
- Provide helpful defaults

## Best Practices

### When implementing features:
- Check existing patterns first
- Reuse components and utilities
- Follow naming conventions
- Keep functions focused and testable
- Document complex logic

### When working with agents:
- Respect agent boundaries
- Use appropriate agent for each task
- Follow agent communication patterns
- Maintain agent context

### When handling errors:
```javascript
try {
  // Operation
} catch (error) {
  console.error(`Error in ${operation}:`, error);
  // Provide helpful error message
  throw new Error(`Failed to ${operation}: ${error.message}`);
}
```

## Git & GitHub Integration

### Commit Conventions
- Use conventional commits: `feat:`, `fix:`, `docs:`, `chore:`, etc.
- Reference story ID: `feat: implement IDE detection [Story 2.1]`
- Keep commits atomic and focused

### GitHub CLI Usage
- Ensure authenticated: `gh auth status`
- Use for PR creation: `gh pr create`
- Check org access: `gh api user/memberships`

<!-- AIOS-MANAGED-START: aios-patterns -->
## AIOS-Specific Patterns

### Working with Templates
```javascript
const template = await loadTemplate('template-name');
const rendered = await renderTemplate(template, context);
```

### Agent Command Handling
```javascript
if (command.startsWith('*')) {
  const agentCommand = command.substring(1);
  await executeAgentCommand(agentCommand, args);
}
```

### Story Updates
```javascript
// Update story progress
const story = await loadStory(storyId);
story.updateTask(taskId, { status: 'completed' });
await story.save();
```
<!-- AIOS-MANAGED-END: aios-patterns -->

## Environment Setup

### Required Tools
- Node.js 18+
- GitHub CLI
- Git
- Your preferred package manager (npm/yarn/pnpm)

### Configuration Files
- `.aios/config.yaml` - Framework configuration
- `.env` - Environment variables
- `aios.config.js` - Project-specific settings

<!-- AIOS-MANAGED-START: common-commands -->
## Common Commands

### AIOS Master Commands
- `*help` - Show available commands
- `*create-story` - Create new story
- `*task {name}` - Execute specific task
- `*workflow {name}` - Run workflow

### Development Commands
- `npm run dev` - Start development
- `npm test` - Run tests
- `npm run lint` - Check code style
- `npm run build` - Build project
<!-- AIOS-MANAGED-END: common-commands -->

## Debugging

### Enable Debug Mode
```bash
export AIOS_DEBUG=true
```

### View Agent Logs
```bash
tail -f .aios/logs/agent.log
```

### Trace Workflow Execution
```bash
npm run trace -- workflow-name
```

## Claude Code Specific Configuration

### Performance Optimization
- Prefer batched tool calls when possible for better performance
- Use parallel execution for independent operations
- Cache frequently accessed data in memory during sessions

### Tool Usage Guidelines
- Always use the Grep tool for searching, never `grep` or `rg` in bash
- Use the Task tool for complex multi-step operations
- Batch file reads/writes when processing multiple files
- Prefer editing existing files over creating new ones

### Session Management
- Track story progress throughout the session
- Update checkboxes immediately after completing tasks
- Maintain context of the current story being worked on
- Save important state before long-running operations

### Error Recovery
- Always provide recovery suggestions for failures
- Include error context in messages to user
- Suggest rollback procedures when appropriate
- Document any manual fixes required

### Testing Strategy
- Run tests incrementally during development
- Always verify lint and typecheck before marking complete
- Test edge cases for each new feature
- Document test scenarios in story files

### Documentation
- Update relevant docs when changing functionality
- Include code examples in documentation
- Keep README synchronized with actual behavior
- Document breaking changes prominently

---
*Synkra AIOS Claude Code Configuration v2.0*

рџ“„ **CLAUDE.md - Synkra AIOS Development Rules for Claude Code v2.0**

Este Г© o arquivo de configuraГ§ГЈo global que define como trabalhar com o Synkra AIOS. Aqui estГЎ um resumo estruturado:

## рџ¤– Sistema de Agentes
- **AtivaГ§ГЈo**: `@dev`, `@qa`, `@architect`, `@pm`, `@po`, `@sm`, `@analyst`
- **Master Agent**: `@aios-master`
- **Comandos**: `*help`, `*create-story`, `*task`, `*exit`

## рџ“– Metodologia de Desenvolvimento
### Story-Driven Development
1. Trabalhar a partir de stories em `docs/stories/`
2. Atualizar progresso: `[ ]` в†’ `[x]`
3. Rastrear mudanГ§as na seГ§ГЈo File List
4. Seguir critГ©rios de aceitaГ§ГЈo

## рџ“Ѓ Estrutura do Framework
```
aios-core/
в”њв”Ђв”Ђ agents/      # DefiniГ§Гµes de personas
в”њв”Ђв”Ђ tasks/       # Workflows executГЎveis
в”њв”Ђв”Ђ workflows/   # DefiniГ§Гµes multi-step
в”њв”Ђв”Ђ templates/   # Templates de documentos/cГіdigo
в”њв”Ђв”Ђ checklists/  # ValidaГ§ГЈo e revisГЈo
в””в”Ђв”Ђ rules/       # Regras do framework

docs/
в”њв”Ђв”Ђ stories/     # Stories de desenvolvimento
в”њв”Ђв”Ђ prd/         # Documentos de requisitos
в”њв”Ђв”Ђ architecture/# DocumentaГ§ГЈo arquitetural
в””в”Ђв”Ђ guides/      # Guias
```

## вљ™пёЏ ConfiguraГ§ГЈo Claude Code
- вњ… Usar ferramentas nativas (Grep, Read, Write, Edit)
- вњ… Batched tool calls para performance
- вњ… ExecuГ§ГЈo paralela quando possГ­vel
- вњ… Rastrear progresso de stories
- вњ… Testes incrementais durante desenvolvimento

## рџ“ќ PadrГµes de CГіdigo
- CГіdigo limpo e auto-documentado
- Tratamento abrangente de erros
- Testes unitГЎrios para novas funcionalidades
- TypeScript/JavaScript best practices

O arquivo estГЎ completo e carregado! Pronto para trabalhar com AIOS. рџљЂ
